---
title: Oscillation Calculator
description: An interactive look at how neutrinos change flavor as they travel, from the two-flavor picture to the full three-flavor PMNS framework.
toc: false
execute:
  echo: false
---

The two sections below let you build intuition interactively:

- **Probability vs neutrino energy $E_\nu$ at a fixed baseline $L$** (left): this is
  what a real experiment with a fixed detector distance actually measures, the
  oscillation pattern imprinted on the energy spectrum.
- **Probability vs baseline $L$ at a fixed energy** (right): the complementary view,
  showing how the flavor composition develops as the neutrino travels.

Start with the two-flavor model for the core idea, then explore the realistic
three-flavor picture that the long-baseline experiments (T2K, NOvA, DUNE,
Hyper-Kamiokande) actually measure.

## The two-flavor picture {#sec-two-flavor .unnumbered}

With only two flavors, oscillation is governed by a single mixing angle $\theta$ and
a single mass-squared splitting $\Delta m^2$. The probability that a neutrino born
as flavor $\alpha$ is detected as the *other* flavor after travelling a baseline $L$
with energy $E$ is

$$
P_{\alpha \to \beta} = \sin^2(2\theta)\,\sin^2\!\left(1.267\,\frac{\Delta m^2\,[\text{eV}^2]\;L\,[\text{km}]}{E\,[\text{GeV}]}\right),
$$

and the survival probability is $P_{\alpha\to\alpha} = 1 - P_{\alpha\to\beta}$. Two
things set the picture: $\sin^2(2\theta)$ fixes the *amplitude* of the oscillation
(how much flavor can be converted), and $\Delta m^2 / E$ fixes the *wavelength*. Pick
an experiment preset, then tweak the sliders.

```{ojs}
osc = {
  const cx   = (re, im = 0) => ({re, im});
  const cadd = (a, b) => ({re: a.re + b.re, im: a.im + b.im});
  const cmul = (a, b) => ({re: a.re * b.re - a.im * b.im, im: a.re * b.im + a.im * b.re});
  const cconj = (a) => ({re: a.re, im: -a.im});
  const cexp = (t) => ({re: Math.cos(t), im: Math.sin(t)}); // e^{i t}
  const K = 1.267; // for Δm² in eV², L in km, E in GeV

  // Two-flavor transition (appearance) probability. E in GeV.
  function P2(L, E, dm2, s2t) {
    return s2t * Math.sin(K * dm2 * L / E) ** 2;
  }

  // Standard PMNS matrix (rows = e,μ,τ ; cols = mass states 1,2,3) as complex entries.
  function pmns(th12, th13, th23, dcp) {
    const s12 = Math.sin(th12), c12 = Math.cos(th12);
    const s13 = Math.sin(th13), c13 = Math.cos(th13);
    const s23 = Math.sin(th23), c23 = Math.cos(th23);
    const em = cexp(-dcp), ep = cexp(dcp);
    return [
      [cx(c12 * c13), cx(s12 * c13), cmul(cx(s13), em)],
      [cadd(cx(-s12 * c23), cmul(cx(-c12 * s23 * s13), ep)),
       cadd(cx(c12 * c23),  cmul(cx(-s12 * s23 * s13), ep)),
       cx(s23 * c13)],
      [cadd(cx(s12 * s23),  cmul(cx(-c12 * c23 * s13), ep)),
       cadd(cx(-c12 * s23), cmul(cx(-s12 * c23 * s13), ep)),
       cx(c23 * c13)]
    ];
  }

  // Three-flavor vacuum oscillation P(ν_a → ν_b). a,b ∈ {0:e,1:μ,2:τ}. E in GeV.
  function P3(a, b, L, E, p) {
    const U = pmns(p.th12, p.th13, p.th23, p.anti ? -p.dcp : p.dcp);
    let P = a === b ? 1 : 0;
    const pairs = [[1, 0, p.dm21], [2, 0, p.dm31], [2, 1, p.dm31 - p.dm21]];
    for (const [i, j, dm] of pairs) {
      const D = K * dm * L / E;
      const term = cmul(cmul(cconj(U[a][i]), U[b][i]), cmul(U[a][j], cconj(U[b][j])));
      P += -4 * term.re * Math.sin(D) ** 2 + 2 * term.im * Math.sin(2 * D);
    }
    return P;
  }

  return {P2, P3};
}

// Energy is stored internally in GeV (the 1.267 factor needs GeV) but shown in MeV.
toMeV = (Egev) => Egev * 1000
fmtMeV = (Egev) => `${+(Egev * 1000).toPrecision(3)}`
// A round slider step (1/2/5 × 10^k) ~ span/200, so preset defaults land on clean values.
niceStep = (span) => {
  const mag = 10 ** Math.floor(Math.log10(span / 200));
  const n = span / 200 / mag;
  return (n < 1.5 ? 1 : n < 3 ? 2 : n < 7 ? 5 : 10) * mag;
}
// Two-line plot title: bold heading on top, parameter value (no brackets) below.
plotTitle = (main, sub) => html`<span style="display:block; line-height:1.25">${main}<br>${sub}</span>`

// Lay a set of Inputs out in a responsive multi-column grid instead of one tall stack.
// Widen the inputs (and trim the label column) so the numeric value boxes show the
// full number rather than truncating to "0....".
gridForm = (inputs) => {
  const form = Inputs.form(inputs);
  form.style.display = "grid";
  form.style.gridTemplateColumns = "repeat(auto-fit, minmax(340px, 1fr))";
  form.style.columnGap = "28px";
  form.style.rowGap = "4px";
  form.style.setProperty("--label-width", "108px");
  form.style.setProperty("--input-width", "210px");
  // Drive a --pct custom property on each range so the blue fill renders in every
  // browser (WebKit does not paint the lower track from accent-color).
  form.querySelectorAll('input[type="range"]').forEach((inp) => {
    const paint = () => {
      const min = +inp.min, max = +inp.max;
      inp.style.setProperty("--pct", (max > min ? (100 * (+inp.value - min)) / (max - min) : 0) + "%");
    };
    paint();
    inp.addEventListener("input", paint);
  });
  return form;
}

// Render two plots side by side, each boxed at a fixed width so a long title cannot
// stretch the figure and force a wrap. Wraps to a single column on narrow screens.
panelW = (w) => Math.max(300, Math.min(620, Math.floor(((w || 900) - 40) / 2)))
panel = (fig, w) => html`<div style="width:${w}px; max-width:100%; overflow:hidden">${fig}</div>`
sidebyside = (figA, figB, w) => html`<div style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:24px; justify-content:center; align-items:flex-start">${panel(figA, w)}${panel(figB, w)}</div>`
```

```{ojs}
html`<style>
#quarto-document-content input[type="range"] {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  appearance: none;
  height: 6px;
  border-radius: 3px;
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #1f77b4 var(--pct, 0%), #cfd3d6 var(--pct, 0%));
  cursor: pointer;
}
#quarto-document-content input[type="range"]::-webkit-slider-thumb {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  appearance: none;
  width: 15px; height: 15px;
  margin-top: -5px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #1f77b4;
  border: 2px solid #fff;
  box-shadow: 0 0 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35);
}
#quarto-document-content input[type="range"]::-moz-range-track {
  height: 6px; border-radius: 3px; background: transparent;
}
#quarto-document-content input[type="range"]::-moz-range-progress {
  height: 6px; border-radius: 3px; background: #1f77b4;
}
#quarto-document-content input[type="range"]::-moz-range-thumb {
  width: 13px; height: 13px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: #1f77b4; border: 2px solid #fff; cursor: pointer;
}
</style>`
```

```{ojs}
tf_presets = [
  {name: "DUNE: accelerator νμ",  L: 1300, E: 2.5,   Lmax: 3000, Emin: 0.2,    Emax: 8,    dm2: 2.5e-3, s2t: 0.97},
  {name: "T2K / Hyper-K: accelerator νμ", L: 295, E: 0.6, Lmax: 1200, Emin: 0.1, Emax: 3, dm2: 2.5e-3, s2t: 1.0},
  {name: "NOvA: accelerator νμ",  L: 810,  E: 2.0,   Lmax: 2000, Emin: 0.2,    Emax: 6,    dm2: 2.5e-3, s2t: 1.0},
  {name: "KamLAND: reactor ν̄e",  L: 180,  E: 0.004, Lmax: 400,  Emin: 0.0015, Emax: 0.01, dm2: 7.5e-5, s2t: 0.85},
  {name: "Daya Bay: reactor θ13", L: 1.6,  E: 0.004, Lmax: 5,    Emin: 0.0015, Emax: 0.01, dm2: 2.5e-3, s2t: 0.085}
]
viewof tf_pre = Inputs.select(tf_presets, {label: "Experiment preset", format: d => d.name})
```

```{ojs}
viewof tf = {
  const eStep = niceStep(toMeV(tf_pre.Emax - tf_pre.Emin));
  const eMin = Math.floor(toMeV(tf_pre.Emin) / eStep) * eStep;
  const eMax = Math.ceil(toMeV(tf_pre.Emax) / eStep) * eStep;
  return gridForm({
    s2t:    Inputs.range([0, 1],     {value: tf_pre.s2t,             step: 0.01, label: "sin²(2θ)"}),
    logdm2: Inputs.range([-4.5, -2], {value: Math.log10(tf_pre.dm2), step: 0.01, label: "log₁₀ Δm² [eV²]"}),
    L:      Inputs.range([0, tf_pre.Lmax], {value: tf_pre.L,        step: niceStep(tf_pre.Lmax), label: "Baseline L [km]"}),
    E:      Inputs.range([eMin, eMax],     {value: toMeV(tf_pre.E), step: eStep,                 label: "Energy E [MeV]"})
  });
}
tf_s2t = tf.s2t
tf_L = tf.L
tf_E = tf.E / 1000  // GeV for the physics
tf_dm2 = 10 ** tf.logdm2
```

```{ojs}
md`**Settings:** sin²(2θ) = ${tf_s2t.toFixed(2)}, Δm² = ${tf_dm2.toExponential(2)} eV². At L = ${tf_L.toFixed(0)} km and E = ${fmtMeV(tf_E)} MeV the survival probability is **${(1 - osc.P2(tf_L, tf_E, tf_dm2, tf_s2t)).toFixed(3)}**.`
```

```{ojs}
tf_dataE = {
  const out = [], n = 600;
  for (let k = 0; k <= n; k++) {
    const Eg = tf_pre.Emin + (tf_pre.Emax - tf_pre.Emin) * k / n; // GeV
    const app = osc.P2(tf_L, Eg, tf_dm2, tf_s2t);
    out.push({E: toMeV(Eg), P: 1 - app, ch: "survival (να→να)"});
    out.push({E: toMeV(Eg), P: app,     ch: "appearance (να→νβ)"});
  }
  return out;
}
tf_dataL = {
  const out = [], n = 600;
  for (let k = 0; k <= n; k++) {
    const L = tf_pre.Lmax * k / n;
    const app = osc.P2(L, tf_E, tf_dm2, tf_s2t);
    out.push({L, P: 1 - app, ch: "survival (να→να)"});
    out.push({L, P: app,     ch: "appearance (να→νβ)"});
  }
  return out;
}
tf_color = ({legend: true, domain: ["survival (να→να)", "appearance (να→νβ)"], range: ["#1f77b4", "#d62728"]})
{
  const w = panelW(width);
  return sidebyside(
    Plot.plot({
      width: w, height: 330, marginLeft: 48,
      title: plotTitle("Probability vs neutrino energy", `L = ${tf_L.toFixed(0)} km`),
      x: {label: "Neutrino energy Eν (MeV) →"},
      y: {label: "↑ Probability", domain: [0, 1], grid: true},
      color: tf_color,
      marks: [
        Plot.ruleY([0]),
        Plot.line(tf_dataE, {x: "E", y: "P", stroke: "ch", strokeWidth: 2}),
        Plot.ruleX([toMeV(tf_E)], {stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "5 4"})
      ]
    }),
    Plot.plot({
      width: w, height: 330, marginLeft: 48,
      title: plotTitle("Probability vs baseline", `E = ${fmtMeV(tf_E)} MeV`),
      x: {label: "Baseline L (km) →"},
      y: {label: "↑ Probability", domain: [0, 1], grid: true},
      color: tf_color,
      marks: [
        Plot.ruleY([0]),
        Plot.line(tf_dataL, {x: "L", y: "P", stroke: "ch", strokeWidth: 2}),
        Plot.ruleX([tf_L], {stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "5 4"})
      ]
    }),
    w
  );
}
```

The energy view (left) is the one to study: an experiment sits at a *fixed* baseline
and reconstructs the neutrino energy event by event, so the dip in the survival curve
is the oscillation signal it measures. The first (deepest) dip, the "oscillation
maximum", sits at $E \approx 0.8\,\Delta m^2\,L$, which is exactly why each beam
energy is matched to its baseline. Set `sin²(2θ) = 1` and the dip reaches zero (full
conversion); lower it and the dip becomes shallower.

## The three-flavor picture {#sec-three-flavor .unnumbered}

Nature has three neutrino flavors, and the full description uses the **PMNS matrix**
$U$, parameterized by three mixing angles ($\theta_{12}, \theta_{13}, \theta_{23}$),
two independent mass splittings ($\Delta m^2_{21}, \Delta m^2_{31}$), and a
**CP-violating phase $\delta_{CP}$**. The vacuum oscillation probability is

$$
P_{\alpha\to\beta} = \delta_{\alpha\beta}
- 4\sum_{i>j}\mathrm{Re}\!\left(U^*_{\alpha i}U_{\beta i}U_{\alpha j}U^*_{\beta j}\right)\sin^2\Delta_{ij}
+ 2\sum_{i>j}\mathrm{Im}\!\left(U^*_{\alpha i}U_{\beta i}U_{\alpha j}U^*_{\beta j}\right)\sin 2\Delta_{ij},
$$

with $\Delta_{ij} = 1.267\,\Delta m^2_{ij}\,L/E$. The experiment preset sets the
baseline and energy; the mixing parameters start from current global-fit values and
stay put when you change preset. The plots show all three channels for a $\nu_\mu$
beam: the survival ($\nu_\mu\to\nu_\mu$) plus the two appearance channels. The
$\nu_\mu\to\nu_e$ appearance channel is the one that carries the CP signal.

```{ojs}
th_presets = [
  {name: "DUNE",               L: 1300, E: 2.5,   Lmax: 3000, Emin: 0.2,    Emax: 8},
  {name: "T2K / Hyper-K",      L: 295,  E: 0.6,   Lmax: 1200, Emin: 0.1,    Emax: 3},
  {name: "NOvA",               L: 810,  E: 2.0,   Lmax: 2000, Emin: 0.2,    Emax: 6},
  {name: "JUNO: reactor ν̄e",  L: 53,   E: 0.004, Lmax: 120,  Emin: 0.0015, Emax: 0.01}
]
viewof th_pre  = Inputs.select(th_presets, {label: "Experiment preset", format: d => d.name})
viewof th_anti = Inputs.toggle({label: "Antineutrinos (flip δ_CP sign)", value: false})
```

```{ojs}
// Baseline & energy snap to the preset; mixing parameters persist across presets.
viewof th_geo = {
  const eStep = niceStep(toMeV(th_pre.Emax - th_pre.Emin));
  const eMin = Math.floor(toMeV(th_pre.Emin) / eStep) * eStep;
  const eMax = Math.ceil(toMeV(th_pre.Emax) / eStep) * eStep;
  return gridForm({
    L: Inputs.range([0, th_pre.Lmax], {value: th_pre.L,        step: niceStep(th_pre.Lmax), label: "Baseline L [km]"}),
    E: Inputs.range([eMin, eMax],     {value: toMeV(th_pre.E), step: eStep,                 label: "Energy E [MeV]"})
  });
}
viewof th_mix = gridForm({
  t12:     Inputs.range([0, 45],     {value: 33.4, step: 0.1, label: "θ₁₂ [°]"}),
  t13:     Inputs.range([0, 15],     {value: 8.6,  step: 0.1, label: "θ₁₃ [°]"}),
  t23:     Inputs.range([20, 70],    {value: 49.0, step: 0.1, label: "θ₂₃ [°]"}),
  dcp:     Inputs.range([-180, 180], {value: -90,  step: 1,   label: "δ_CP [°]"}),
  logdm21: Inputs.range([-5, -3.5],  {value: Math.log10(7.4e-5), step: 0.01, label: "log₁₀ Δm²₂₁ [eV²]"}),
  logdm31: Inputs.range([-3, -2],    {value: Math.log10(2.5e-3), step: 0.01, label: "log₁₀ Δm²₃₁ [eV²]"})
})
th_L = th_geo.L
th_E = th_geo.E / 1000  // GeV for the physics
th_params = ({
  th12: th_mix.t12 * Math.PI / 180, th13: th_mix.t13 * Math.PI / 180, th23: th_mix.t23 * Math.PI / 180,
  dcp: th_mix.dcp * Math.PI / 180, dm21: 10 ** th_mix.logdm21, dm31: 10 ** th_mix.logdm31, anti: th_anti
})
```

```{ojs}
md`**Settings:** δ_CP = ${th_mix.dcp}°${th_anti ? " (antineutrino mode)" : ""}. At L = ${th_L.toFixed(0)} km and E = ${fmtMeV(th_E)} MeV the νμ→νe appearance probability is **${osc.P3(1, 0, th_L, th_E, th_params).toFixed(4)}**.`
```

```{ojs}
th_lab = th_anti ? ["ν̄μ→ν̄e", "ν̄μ→ν̄μ", "ν̄μ→ν̄τ"] : ["νμ→νe", "νμ→νμ", "νμ→ντ"]
th_dataE = {
  const out = [], n = 700;
  for (let k = 0; k <= n; k++) {
    const Eg = th_pre.Emin + (th_pre.Emax - th_pre.Emin) * k / n; // GeV
    out.push({E: toMeV(Eg), P: osc.P3(1, 0, th_L, Eg, th_params), ch: th_lab[0]});
    out.push({E: toMeV(Eg), P: osc.P3(1, 1, th_L, Eg, th_params), ch: th_lab[1]});
    out.push({E: toMeV(Eg), P: osc.P3(1, 2, th_L, Eg, th_params), ch: th_lab[2]});
  }
  return out;
}
th_dataL = {
  const out = [], n = 700;
  for (let k = 0; k <= n; k++) {
    const L = th_pre.Lmax * k / n;
    out.push({L, P: osc.P3(1, 0, L, th_E, th_params), ch: th_lab[0]});
    out.push({L, P: osc.P3(1, 1, L, th_E, th_params), ch: th_lab[1]});
    out.push({L, P: osc.P3(1, 2, L, th_E, th_params), ch: th_lab[2]});
  }
  return out;
}
th_color = ({legend: true, domain: th_lab, range: ["#d62728", "#1f77b4", "#2ca02c"]})
{
  const w = panelW(width);
  return sidebyside(
    Plot.plot({
      width: w, height: 350, marginLeft: 48,
      title: plotTitle("Probability vs neutrino energy", `L = ${th_L.toFixed(0)} km`),
      x: {label: "Neutrino energy Eν (MeV) →"},
      y: {label: "↑ Probability", domain: [0, 1], grid: true},
      color: th_color,
      marks: [
        Plot.ruleY([0]),
        Plot.line(th_dataE, {x: "E", y: "P", stroke: "ch", strokeWidth: 2}),
        Plot.ruleX([toMeV(th_E)], {stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "5 4"})
      ]
    }),
    Plot.plot({
      width: w, height: 350, marginLeft: 48,
      title: plotTitle("Probability vs baseline", `E = ${fmtMeV(th_E)} MeV`),
      x: {label: "Baseline L (km) →"},
      y: {label: "↑ Probability", domain: [0, 1], grid: true},
      color: th_color,
      marks: [
        Plot.ruleY([0]),
        Plot.line(th_dataL, {x: "L", y: "P", stroke: "ch", strokeWidth: 2}),
        Plot.ruleX([th_L], {stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "5 4"})
      ]
    }),
    w
  );
}
```

The $\nu_\mu\to\nu_e$ appearance channel is small (a few percent) but it is the
keystone measurement for the next generation of experiments. On the energy plot, drag
the $\delta_{CP}$ slider and watch the $\nu_\mu\to\nu_e$ spectrum shift, then flip the
**antineutrino** toggle and watch it shift the *other* way. That difference between
neutrinos and antineutrinos is **CP violation in the lepton sector**, and measuring
it is one of the headline goals of DUNE and Hyper-Kamiokande. Notice too that
$\theta_{13}$ controls how large the appearance can ever get (set it to zero and the
channel essentially vanishes), while $\theta_{23}$ governs the depth of the
$\nu_\mu\to\nu_\mu$ survival dip.

::: {.callout-note}
This calculator uses **vacuum** oscillation. Real long-baseline neutrinos travel
through the Earth, where coherent forward scattering off electrons (the MSW matter
effect) modifies the probabilities. The vacuum curves capture the essential physics
and the role of every parameter, but are not a substitute for a full matter-effect
calculation.
:::
